content.text: Circuit Tracks features two 1/4" (6.35mm) audio inputs for external sources, stereo 1/4" line outputs for speakers or mixers, and a 3.5mm headphone output.

rows: Sums to mono when used alone

content.text: Two 1/4" (6.35mm) mono inputs (Input 1 and Input 2) accept line-level audio from synthesizers, drum machines, phones, tablets, or other instruments.

content.title: Use Cases
content.text: Connect external synths to process through Circuit Tracks' effects, a phone for backing tracks, or another groovebox for jamming together.
External audio routed through the inputs can be:

items: Mixed with Circuit Tracks' internal sounds
Processed through the delay and reverb effects
Adjusted in volume via the mixer

content.text: External audio cannot be recorded or sampled directly - it's for live mixing and effects processing only.
The main stereo outputs use separate 1/4" (6.35mm) jacks for left and right channels. Connect to mixers, audio interfaces, or powered speakers.

content.title: Mono Output
content.text: When only the L/Mono output is connected, the stereo signal is automatically summed to mono. Use both outputs for stereo.
The 3.5mm stereo headphone output provides a full mix of all internal tracks plus any external audio routed through the inputs. Volume is controlled by the Master Volume knob.
The line outputs and headphone output share the same stereo mix - there's no separate cue/monitor bus.

items: Keep individual track levels moderate to avoid digital clipping
Use the Master Volume knob for final output level
Watch the visual level meter in the mixer view
The Master Filter at center position is bypassed (no effect)
